description: For every Partial/Missing requirement API identified in W1-T02, assigns owner layer, fallback strategy, blocker level, and closure condition. Ensures zero 'unknown state' requirement items remain.

openGaps: No apiVersion field in geometry payload contract.
No diagnostics object (candidateCount/filteredCount/costMs) in returned geometry payload.
gapItems.ownerRationale: The single-entry function resolveEngineGeometryPayload owns this surface. All geometry enrichment is runtime-side by W1-T03 single-entry rule; engine capability.spatial.createHitGeometryPayload is the backing call.

closureApproach.description: Add a named wrapper resolveHitGeometryV2 in engine-bridge/engine.ts that calls resolveEngineGeometryPayload and appends the apiVersion and diagnostics fields to the return value. The wrapper satisfies the PRD name contract without modifying engine internals.
closureApproach.prerequisite: W1-T03 frozen spec ( matrix/transform chain is stable before enriching payload shape.

gapItems.blockerRationale: Hit results work today via the existing partial mapping. The missing diagnostics and apiVersion fields are observability improvements, not functional blockers for P0 hit-testing.
gapItems.closureCondition: resolveHitGeometryV2 is exported from engine-bridge/engine.ts, includes apiVersion, and returns a diagnostics object with at minimum candidateCount and costMs.

openGaps: Current engine.invalidate accepts a single region + reason; no accepted/dropped region response.
No mergedRegionCount in public return contract.
No fallbackFullRedraw flag in invalidate result.
gapItems.ownerRationale: engine.invalidate is the closest implementation in EngineHandle. Region merging and fallback-full-redraw logic belong in the engine orchestration layer, not in the runtime bridge, to keep the bridge thin.

closureApproach.description: Extend EngineHandle.invalidate (or add an overload invalidateRegions) to accept a regions array and return {accepted, dropped, mergedRegionCount, fallbackFullRedraw}. This is a stable-path addition (does not break existing callers).
closureApproach.prerequisite: Backend tier policy (done, W1-T01) defines Tier1 must honour region  no further discovery needed.

gapItems.blockerRationale: Invalidation today triggers full redraws conservatively; the missing structure is an optimisation gate rather than a functional outage. No feature is blocked.
gapItems.closureCondition: EngineHandle exposes invalidateRegions (or an enriched invalidate overload) that returns mergedRegionCount and fallbackFullRedraw; runtime bridge wraps it as invalidateSceneRegions.

openGaps: No public syncSceneDelta entry with deterministic response fields.
Revision mismatch not exposed as a normalized error shape.
gapItems.ownerRationale: Scene delta sync is orchestrated in the runtime bridge via batchUpdateGraph/updateGraph calls. The bridge owns the delta decision logic (what to skip, when to fall back to full snapshot). Engine only receives the final graph operation.

closureApproach.description: Add syncSceneDelta in engine-bridge/engine.ts as a named adapter over batchUpdateGraph. It should compute skippedPatchCount from the delta diff, detect revision mismatch, and return a structured {applied, skippedPatchCount, revisionMismatch} response. Revision mismatch should throw/return a typed error shape.
closureApproach.prerequisite: Document model patch contract stability (W1-T06 GAP-03 depends on M2-R11/R12 passing clean before adding the mismatch error shape).

gapItems.blockerRationale: Revision mismatch with no structured error forces callers to implement ad-hoc recovery; this is a source of data-loss risk during concurrent edits. Highest-priority gap in this list.
gapItems.closureCondition: syncSceneDelta is exported from engine-bridge, returns {applied, skippedPatchCount}, and throws/returns a typed RevisionMismatchError when revision ordering is violated.
gapItems.targetPhase: Phase 1 (M1-3 incremental sync  must land before Phase 2 collaboration features

openGaps: No interactionPhase/source fields in setView input.
No clamped marker in viewport result.
No anchorClamp/scaleClamp diagnostics in viewport commit response.
gapItems.ownerRationale: setView is an EngineHandle operation; the interactionPhase/source context and clamp diagnostics are runtime-bridge responsibilities since the engine does not model interaction phases.

closureApproach.description: Add commitViewportState in engine-bridge/engine.ts that wraps setView. The wrapper accepts {viewport, interactionPhase, source} input, calls setView, then reads back clamped state from getView to produce {committed, clamped, anchorClamp, scaleClamp} output.
closureApproach.prerequisite: W1-T03 frozen viewport coordinate system ( clamping semantics derivable from current spec.

gapItems.blockerRationale: Viewport commit works today via setView. The missing clamped/source fields are needed for diagnostics and animation interpolation polish; not blocking core editing.
gapItems.closureCondition: commitViewportState is exported from engine-bridge, accepts interactionPhase, and returns {committed, clamped} at minimum; anchorClamp/scaleClamp may be deferred to Phase 2.

openGaps: No single API merging engine diagnostics and runtime scheduler diagnostics.
queue/throttle/coalesced/inFlight fields exist in runtime bridge only, not in engine public contract.
Fallback counters and invalidation reason not normalized in one contract.
gapItems.ownerRationale: A unified diagnostics snapshot requires merging engine.getDiagnostics output (engine-side) with scheduler/throttle state (runtime-bridge-side). The bridge is the only layer that can see both; engine must not import bridge.

closureApproach.description: Add getRuntimeDiagnosticsSnapshot in engine-bridge/engine.ts that calls engine.getDiagnostics(), reads the runtime bridge scheduler diagnostics, and merges them into a single normalized DiagnosticsSnapshot type. Include apiVersion field. Fallback counter fields may be sourced from the existing runtime diagnostics already visible in playground status bar.
closureApproach.prerequisite: both sources are already readable; this is a composition task only.

gapItems.blockerRationale: Diagnostics are observability-only; no user-facing feature is blocked. Playground status bar already surfaces most of these fields informally.
gapItems.closureCondition: getRuntimeDiagnosticsSnapshot is exported from engine-bridge, returns a typed DiagnosticsSnapshot with engine + scheduler sections, and includes apiVersion.
gapItems.targetPhase: Phase 1 (any M1 milestone, low priority among P1 items)

closureSummary.prioritisationRationale: GAP-03 (syncSceneDelta revision mismatch) is the only high-blocker and the only data-safety risk. GAP-01 and GAP-04 are functional completeness. GAP-02 and GAP-05 are optimisation/observability.

governanceNotes: All adapter-wrapper additions in engine-bridge/engine.ts must follow W1-T03 single-entry  no new geometry/transform logic outside the frozen single-entry functions.
Each wrapper must include a leading intent comment and full JSDoc @param/@returns per repo standards.
AI-TEMP tags are required for any interim compat shim that precedes the real engine extension.
